downgraded.claim: The cerebellum contributes to executive function and language via crossed cerebro-cerebellar loops, beyond timing.
Resting-state fMRI shows the cerebellum is functionally connected with prefrontal and parietal association cortices supporting cognition.

anchors.title: Buckner 2013 The Cerebellum and Cognitive Function

downgraded.claim: Cerebellar lesions produce Cerebellar Cognitive Affective Syndrome (CCAS) characterized by deficits in executive function, visuospatial cognition, language, and affect regulation.
The cerebellum participates in distributed cognitive networks through reciprocal connectivity with prefrontal cortex.
